items.question: What is an ingrown toenail and how is it treated?
items.answer: An ingrown toenail occurs when the edges of the nail grow into the skin, causing pain and inflammation. Our treatment includes correction using bracing systems (titanium wire) or partial resection, avoiding complete nail removal.

items.question: Why are custom orthotics necessary?
items.answer: Custom orthotics correct foot posture, redistribute pressure, and prevent back and knee pain. They are made based on an exact cast of your foot.

items.question: What does diabetic foot care involve?
items.answer: People with diabetes have reduced foot sensitivity. We offer specialized medical pedicures to prevent wounds, infections, and ulcers that can lead to serious complications.

items.question: How is plantar fasciitis (heel pain) treated?
items.answer: We treat heel pain by combining stretching exercises, the use of custom orthotics, and, in some cases, shock wave therapy to reduce fascia inflammation.
